task_completion.description: Agent created a script to interact with CoppeliaSim via ZMQ Remote API.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Python script is visible in a text editor or terminal, importing the CoppeliaSim ZMQ Remote API and containing logic to build the scene and evaluate lighting.

task_completion.description: lighting_sweep.csv is exported with correct columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file lighting_sweep.csv is visible in /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/ (e.g., via terminal or file manager) and its contents show the header: pose_id, light_x, light_y, light_z, contrast_metric.

task_completion.description: lighting_sweep.csv contains at least 24 data rows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of lighting_sweep.csv show at least 24 rows of evaluated lighting positions and contrast metrics.

task_completion.description: lighting_report.json is exported with required fields and valid data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file lighting_report.json is visible in the exports directory, containing fields: total_positions_tested, best_pose_id, best_x, best_y, best_z, max_contrast_metric, min_contrast_metric. The total_positions_tested must be at least 24.

task_completion.description: optimal_inspection.png is exported showing the vision sensor image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file optimal_inspection.png is visible in the exports directory. If opened, it should show a rendered image of the test target (e.g., a gray cuboid with a white cylinder).

integrity.description: Files were generated by running a script, not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the execution of the Python script. The CSV and JSON files should not be manually typed in a text editor.

integrity.description: The exported PNG is a genuine CoppeliaSim render of the constructed scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The optimal_inspection.png looks like a 3D render of a test target from a downward-facing camera, not a random image from the internet.

integrity.description: The script dynamically calculates the contrast metric and positions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script contains mathematical logic for a hemisphere and standard deviation calculation, rather than hardcoded arrays of fake results.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires evaluating at least 24 candidate positions. The expected output files are: /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/lighting_sweep.csv, /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/lighting_report.json, and /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/optimal_inspection.png.
